headline: Mumbai Local Train Arrives On Time; Entire City Demands An Explanation
summary: Passengers refused to board, convinced it was a trap. Western Railway confirms it was, in fact, the 8:42.
fullBody: Commuters at Borivali station were thrown into unprecedented confusion on Tuesday morning when the 8:42 Churchgate fast local arrived at 8:42 AM — on time, at the correct platform, with working AC in all coaches. Crowds gathered but refused to board for six minutes, with one regular commuter of 22 years, Ramesh Padhye, telling reporters: 'I've been taking this train since 2003. This is not right. Something is very wrong.' Western Railway issued a formal press statement confirming the train was genuine and on time, citing 'a favourable alignment of factors that we cannot fully explain and do not expect to replicate.' By the time passengers accepted the train was real, it had departed. It was three minutes late, which was described as 'a great relief' by all present.
